mission.title: Install and Configure Cluster API (CAPI)
mission.description: Cluster API (CAPI) is a Kubernetes operator that provides declarative APIs and tooling to simplify the provisioning, upgrading, and management of multiple Kubernetes clusters. It is particularly useful for platform operators who need to manage clusters across various infrastructure environments. Key trade-offs include the complexity of setup and the need for familiarity with Kubernetes concepts.

steps.title: Add the Cluster API Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Cluster API Helm repository to your Helm configuration, allowing you to install the necessary components.

```bash

helm repo add cluster-api https://charts.cluster-api.sigs.k8s.io

```

> This command adds the Cluster API Helm chart repository.

steps.title: Install the Cluster API components
steps.description:
This step installs the Cluster API components into your Kubernetes cluster using Helm.

```bash

helm install cluster-api cluster-api/cluster-api --namespace kube-system --version 1.12.4

```

> This installs the Cluster API components in the kube-system namespace.

steps.title: Install the Cluster API Provider
steps.description:
This step installs a specific provider for Cluster API (e.g., AWS, Azure). Replace 'aws' with your desired provider.

```bash

helm install cluster-api-provider-aws cluster-api/cluster-api-provider-aws --namespace kube-system --version 1.12.4

```

> This installs the AWS provider for Cluster API.

steps.title: Create a Cluster Custom Resource
steps.description:
This step creates a Cluster Custom Resource (CR) to define your Kubernetes cluster configuration.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: cluster.x-k8s.io/v1alpha4
kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: my-cluster
  namespace: default
spec:
  infrastructureRef:
    apiVersion: infrastructure.cluster.x-k8s.io/v1alpha4
    kind: AWSCluster
    name: my-cluster
EOF

```

> This creates a new cluster resource named 'my-cluster'.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: cluster.x-k8s.io/v1alpha4
kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: my-cluster
  namespace: default
spec:
  infrastructureRef:
    apiVersion: infrastructure.cluster.x-k8s.io/v1alpha4
    kind: AWSCluster
    name: my-cluster
EOF

steps.title: Verify the Cluster Custom Resource
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the Cluster Custom Resource to ensure it is created successfully.

```bash

kubectl get clusters -A

```

> This command lists all clusters and their statuses.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Cluster API components
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Cluster API components and may lead to data loss. Ensure to backup any important data before proceeding.

```bash

helm uninstall cluster-api --namespace kube-system

helm uninstall cluster-api-provider-aws --namespace kube-system

```

> This command uninstalls the Cluster API components.
> This command uninstalls the AWS provider.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Cluster API components
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure to backup your cluster configurations. This step upgrades the Cluster API components to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get clusters -A -o yaml > cluster-backup.yaml

helm upgrade cluster-api cluster-api/cluster-api --namespace kube-system --version 1.12.4

kubectl get clusters -A

```

> Backup existing cluster configurations.
> This command upgrades the Cluster API components.
> Verify that the clusters are still present and running after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'failed to install CRD'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the CRDs are not properly installed or if there is a version mismatch.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the CRDs are installed correctly and match the version of the Cluster API.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Cluster not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified cluster may not exist or may have been deleted.

**Fix:**
Check the list of clusters with 'kubectl get clusters -A' to ensure the cluster exists.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'unable to connect to the server'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Kubernetes API server is not reachable.

**Fix:**
Check your kubeconfig and ensure that the Kubernetes cluster is running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'provider not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This occurs when the specified provider is not installed or not configured correctly.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the correct provider Helm chart is installed and configured.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new infrastructure providers and improved CRD management.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated legacy API versions for Cluster and Machine resources.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.11.x, ensure to update your CRD definitions to the new API versions.

resolution.summary: You should see a list of clusters with their statuses when running 'kubectl get clusters -A'. Each cluster should show 'Ready' in the status column.
codeSnippets:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: cluster.x-k8s.io/v1alpha4
kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: my-cluster
  namespace: default
spec:
  infrastructureRef:
    apiVersion: infrastructure.cluster.x-k8s.io/v1alpha4
    kind: AWSCluster
    name: my-cluster
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a running Kubernetes cluster and the necessary tools installed.
